sources.label: StockTitan VPG news feed (latest item May 28, 2026; nothing dated Jun 5-9)
VPG to participate in June investor conferences (Noble Capital, East Coast IDEAS) - May 28 PR
Investing.com - VPG Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (May 12 beat, humanoid bookings ramp)
Yahoo Finance - VPG up 56.7% after strong Q1 bookings and raised Q2 outlook

verdicts.rankRationale: No news since Jun 6 and a flat tape today (-0.93%); the massive rerating (+202.6% YTD, ~+100% in 30 days off the May 12 humanoid-driven Q1 beat) was already penalized into the 'crowded/rerated benchmark' bucket and yesterday's hold. Small negative drift only - residual alpha keeps eroding on a crowding-sensitive list, but nothing new justifies a fresh move.
verdicts.bull: Q1 bookings of $102.1M (book-to-bill 1.21) and 17.6% y/y revenue growth show real demand pull-through in precision sensing and current-sense; humanoid shipments expected to more than double in Q2.
verdicts.neutral: Strategically real but fully discovered: a $1.57B small-cap that has tripled YTD now trades on execution of guidance, not on hidden bottleneck value.
verdicts.bear: Any wobble in the Q2 $85-90M revenue guide or the humanoid ramp narrative would hit a stock priced for flawless execution; CFO retirement (Dec 2026) adds a transition wrinkle.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results confirming the humanoid follow-on order for hundreds of units and the $85-90M revenue guide.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding: a +203% YTD small-cap with no new evidence has asymmetric downside if the H2 humanoid production ramp slips.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved VPG down for crowding and the +200% YTD rerating; nothing new since Jun 6 and today's -0.93% drift does not change the setup, so respect the prior move and hold at the lowered rank.
verdicts.bull: Most concrete humanoid revenue proof in the sensor space: $1.0M of humanoid orders in Q1, ~$600K shipped, a fourth developer engaged, 20-30 force sensors per robot, and a stated path to hundreds of robots per week by end of 2026.
verdicts.neutral: The force-sensing thesis is intact and validated, but the May 12 print already converted it from hidden alpha to consensus; current rank fairly reflects relevance minus crowding.
verdicts.bear: Humanoid revenue is still tiny (~$600K/quarter) versus a $1.57B cap; if leading developers delay the late-2026 production ramp, the robotics premium deflates fast.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Confirmation of the expected Q2 follow-on humanoid order for hundreds of units and doubling of humanoid shipments.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Humanoid production timelines slipping into 2027, leaving the stock's robotics narrative unsupported by revenue.
